channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: “I think generative AI is going to get much faster and much better ” — The Fallout creator might be optimistic about the technology's impact on games, but I'm not so sure
item.description: Fallout creator Tim Cain is bullish about the future of generative AI — how do you feel about it?

item.title: The 12 best home office deals to level up your workspace in Best Buy’s Ultimate Upgrade Sale
item.description: I went through the entire sale to find the top upgrades for any home office

item.title: Garmin Instinct E : cette montre outdoor, parfaite pour les sportifs et aventuriers, chute à 179 €
item.description: Pensée pour les randonneurs, traileurs et amateurs d’aventure, la Garmin Instinct E est une montre outdoor robuste, fiable et bien équipée sans exploser votre budget. C’est d’autant plus vrai maintenant que son prix chute à 179 euros contre 299 euros à sa sortie.

item.title: Victrola’s latest turntable is a Record Store Day exclusive — and it wouldn’t be anything without the watchful eye of rock icon Jack White
item.description: Victrola is relaunching its Wave turntable with Jack White's Third Man Records to celebrate Record Store Day 2026.

item.title: ChatGPT, Gemini et Claude seraient meilleurs que les pubs pour vendre un produit : vous avez sûrement déjà été influencé
item.description: Un chatbot qui pousse un produit discrètement fait trois fois mieux qu'un moteur de recherche. Et prévenir l'utilisateur ne le protège presque pas.

item.title: Roblox agrees to a $12 million settlement with Nevada
item.description: Amidst ongoing legal trouble with several states and more than 100 pending lawsuits, this week Roblox announced a $12 million settlement with Nevada, allowing the company to avoid going to trial in this case.Following the agreement, Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford said "this settlement will create a safer environment for our children online, and I hope that it will serve as a bellwether for how online interactive platforms allow our state's youth to use their products." As part of the deal, Roblox has agreed to give $10 million over three years to local children's support programs like the ...

item.title: Avis SFR (2026) : faut-il souscrire aux forfaits mobiles et box internet de l’opérateur au carré rouge avant son rachat ?
item.description: SFR est un opérateur historique en France qui inspire des sentiments mitigés. Certes, l'opérateur au carré rouge s'est nettement amélioré sur le plan technologique avec la 5G+ et le Wi-Fi 7, mais un éventuel rachat par les rivaux pourrait refroidir l'envie de souscrire à un forfait SFR.

item.title: Donut Lab's battery claims reportedly subject of whistleblower complaint
item.description: Startup Donut Lab made a splash at the start of the year with some astonishing — and suspicious — claims about its solid state batteries. Now Finnish newspaper Helsingin Sanomat reports an individual has filed a criminal whistleblower complaint against the company over those claims. Until recently, Lauri Peltola was listed as the Chief Commercial Officer at Nordic Nano — the firm reportedly contracted to handle portions of the manufacturing on Donut's behalf, and which Donut Lab has invested in. He reportedly filed a criminal complaint that Donut Lab’s promises of energy density and longevity ...

item.title: Cisco tells Webex users to patch critical security flaws immediately, as experts find its Wi-Fi boxes may be filling their disks with undeletable data every day
item.description: Four critical flaws were recently patched, and some AP versions are creating undeletable, growing files.

item.title: The Tor Project takes a major step toward launching its mobile VPN with successful Cure53 audit
item.description: The Tor Project has published the results of an independent security audit on its upcoming TorVPN app for Android. Conducted by renowned cybersecurity firm Cure53, the penetration test confirmed the core routing features are robust, while highlighting a few areas for the development team to harden before an official release.

item.title: Republican Mutiny Sinks Trump's Push to Extend Warrantless Surveillance
item.description: A post-midnight revolt in the House sank the White House's efforts to extend Section 702—a spy program the FBI has used to look into members of Congress, protesters, and political donors.

item.title: You might soon be able to buy luxury Gucci-branded Android XR smart glasses, and we dread to think what they’ll cost
item.description: Gucci could become the first mainstream luxury brand to launch smart specs, as the company is targeting 2027 for a pair of Android XR glasses.

item.title: Should my enterprise AI agent do that? NanoClaw and Vercel launch easier agentic policy setting and approval dialogs across 15 messaging apps
item.description: For the past year, early adopters of autonomous AI agents have been forced to play a murky game of chance: keep the agent in a useless sandbox or give it the keys to the kingdom and hope it doesn't hallucinate a catastrophic "delete all" command.To unlock the true utility of an agent—scheduling meetings, triaging emails, or managing cloud infrastructure—users have had to grant these models raw API keys and broad permissions, raising the risk of their systems being disrupted by an accidental agent mistake. That tradeoff ends today. The creators of the open source sandboxed NanoClaw agent framew...

item.title: ‘Project Hail Mary’ Won’t Be Coming to Streaming Any Time Soon
item.description: The film's new IMAX extension is in part due to Amazon agreeing to delay the film's release on Prime Video.

item.title: Ghost orchid in the machine
item.description: Vacuum cleaners, personal massagers, electronic baby rockers, and walking pads: These are the secondhand machines Rachel Youn sources to create their kinetic sculptures. Made with artificial flowers, metal hardware, and these used electronic components, each one possesses a humanlike presence. Slow Burn is made from an artificial orchid, a neck massager, bits of metal that […]...

item.title: The South Korean president is doing quote-post diplomacy
item.description: "This is no different from Comfort Women or the Holocaust," wrote South Korean President Lee Jae-myung on X last week, quoting a post with a video of Israeli Defense Forces soldiers throwing a body off a rooftop in Gaza. The president's post kicked off an internet firestorm for a thousand different reasons, not least because […]

item.title: Peloton, stay in your lane
item.description: This is Optimizer, a weekly newsletter sent every Friday from Verge senior reviewer Victoria Song that dissects and discusses the latest gizmos and potions that swear they're going to change your life. Opt in for Optimizer here. The camera zooms in on two well-formed cheeks clad in white shorts. These buns of steel belong to […]

item.title: With US spy laws set to expire, lawmakers are split over protecting Americans from warrantless surveillance
item.description: Some lawmakers are calling for widespread reforms following years of surveillance scandals and abuses across successive U.S. administrations. But even if the spy law known as Section 702 expires in April, the government's spy powers will not automatically lapse.

item.title: Google’s AI Mode can now help you find products in stock nearby
item.description: Although you can already track hotel prices at the city level, the new update lets you do so for a specific hotel that you're interested in.

item.title: Legendary strategy franchise Heroes of Might and Magic is back in the first new entry in over a decade — 'This is one of those generational games' developer says
item.description: Heroes of Might and Magic is coming back in an all-new entry helmed by a fresh developer.

item.title: 7 new movies and TV shows to watch on Netflix, Prime Video, HBO Max, and more this weekend (April 17)
item.description: From Beef season 2 to Mark Wahlberg's new Prime Video movie, here's what's worth watching this weekend.

item.title: YouTube : des années après le Web, une option de partage indispensable arrive enfin sur mobile
item.description: YouTube continue son travail d'harmonisation entre ses différents supports. Le partage via l'application mobile devient plus pratique. Il était temps.

item.title: Cybertruck : mais pourquoi Elon Musk achète-t-il ses propres voitures ?
item.description: Parmi les 7 071 Cybertrucks immatriculés aux États-Unis au cours du quatrième trimestre 2025, pas moins de 1 279 ont été livrés à des sociétés d'Elon Musk.

item.title: Vers la fin des DVD et Blu-Ray Disney ? L’entreprise ne veut plus s’occuper des supports physiques
item.description: Une vague de licenciements frappe Disney, avec parmi les victimes la division chargée des éditions physiques des films du studio.

item.title: How to watch The Murder Line online from anywhere — it's *FREE*
item.description: A cop must choose between justice and lifelong friendship. Here's how to watch The Murder Line online from anywhere, including for free.

item.title: Activision débranche Warzone Mobile : le jeu a fait un flop sur smartphone
item.description: Clap de fin pour le battle royale d'Activision. Ce 17 avril 2026, les serveurs de Call of Duty Warzone Mobile ont été officiellement et définitivement débranchés. À peine deux ans après son lancement mondial, le titre fait ses adieux, victime d'un public mobile qui n'a jamais vraiment répondu présent.

item.title: « L’IA va tuer l’open source » : l’alerte du populaire projet Cal
item.description: L'émergence de l'IA générative, en particulier dans la programmation d'applications, continue de perturber le marché. D'après Cal, un projet populaire open source, elle représente la mort de l'open source.

item.title: On a essayé de m’arnaquer avec une photo IA et un vocal envoyés par SMS : attention à cette nouvelle mode très piégeuse
item.description: Une photo d'un colis générée avec l'intelligence artificielle, et un vocal d'une dizaine de secondes probablement, lui aussi, fait avec l'IA, le tout envoyé par SMS : voici la nouvelle technique utilisée par les scammeurs pour dérober vos données personnelles. La vigilance est de mise.

item.title: ExoMars : après vingt ans de galères, l’Europe a enfin son rover pour Mars
item.description: Le rover Rosalind Franklin est prêt depuis 2022. Il attend dans un hangar italien que l'Europe lui trouve un chauffeur pour Mars.

item.title: Fnac casse le prix de la Xiaomi Electric Scooter 5 Pro, une trottinette endurante sur 60 km
item.description: Avec son autonomie allant jusqu’à 60 km, la Xiaomi Electric Scooter 5 Pro s’impose comme une trottinette électrique particulièrement adaptée aux trajets du quotidien comme aux déplacements plus longs. Même si elle a depuis été remplacée dans la gamme, elle reste une valeur sûre surtout maintenant qu'elle revient à 399 euros seulement chez la Fnac. ...

item.title: The ‘AI is inevitable’ trap
item.description: In the latest sign of AI silly season, Allbirds, the shoe company, told the world it was now an AI company and briefly managed to septuple its stock price. The Newbird AI story is really just one of a bunch of things this week that made us wonder: have we reached the peak of AI, […]

item.title: Bluesky confirms DDoS attack is cause of continued app outages
item.description: Bluesky has been experiencing ongoing service disruptions since just before 3 a.m. ET. on April 15.

item.title: TCL Says Screw All Those New RGB TVs, Even Its Own
item.description: TCL now has its own RGB TV that it explicitly says offers less value than its new 'super quantum dot' mini LED screens.

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream devs say there was 'a big debate' about whether Miis should fart — 'We really obsessed over getting the sound just right'
item.description: The Tomodachi Life: Living the Dreamcreators have revealed that there was a debate between the development team about whether they should allow Miis to fart.

item.title: MAGA Is Increasingly Convinced the Trump Assassination Attempt Was Staged
item.description: Conspiracy theories about the Butler, Pennsylvania, shooting have ramped up in recent weeks as once steadfast Trump supporters turn on the president.

item.title: Netflix plans to add a vertical video feed, use AI for recommendations
item.description: Netflix is going to launch a TikTok-like vertical video feed within its apps this month, and plans to use AI broadly for content creation and recommendations.

item.title: A Starlink outage left dozens of unmanned US Navy vessels stranded off the California coast — and exposed the Pentagon's worrying reliance on Elon Musk
item.description: 'You accept those vulnerabilities because of the benefits you get from the ubiquity Starlink provides' — and it's the only realistic option right now.

item.title: Samsung finally explains why it stopped making small phones — and has a suggestion for fans of compact handsets
item.description: Samsung stopped making small phones because people needed larger screens, but there's still a relatively compact option to pick from.

item.title: Air New Zealand economy bunk beds are finally coming. How much would you pay for a four-hour nap in the sky?
item.description: Aukland to New York is one of the world’s longest flights. No wonder New Zealand’s national airline wants to make it more comfortable. Picture it: You’re in an economy seat on a 17-hour flight. Would you pay an extra $300 for just four hours lying flat?

item.title: Microsoft experts warn North Korean attackers target macOS users with 'a highly reliable infection chain' to steal passwords, financial data and more — here's how to stay safe
item.description: A Lazarus spinoff is stirring trouble among companies, stealing crypto through fake jobs, Microsoft warns.

item.title: DJI reveals Osmo Pocket 4, upgrading its popular gimbal camera. But theres a big catch.
item.description: DJI has unveiled the Osmo Pocket 4, the next iteration of its popular handheld gimbal camera.

item.title: It's marathon season, and Amazon is slashing prices on best-selling running shoes and watches — up to 55% off Hoka, Nike, Garmin, and Apple
item.description: Prep for marathon season with Amazon's weekend sale on best-selling running shoes and watches, with up to 55% from Hoka, Nike, Garmin, Apple, and more.

item.title: The ‘TMNT: Mutant Mayhem’ Sequel Has Found Its Villains
item.description: Plus, Takashi Yamazak talks Godzilla's attack on New York in 'Minus Zero'.
